concept: Spine / The Back's Line / The Column That Uprights Us
description: The spine: the back's central line, the column standing ('whano') depends on. Completes the back's anatomy with 'rokai' (back) and 'tholau' (bone).
sound_symbolism: Kin in sound to 'rokai' (back): 'ro' the back's own syllable, 'ki' the articulated line, 'li' running through — the column drawn downward in sound.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'shia rokili thenui nai.' (Their spine is straight.) In care: 'mia mua rokili kipona phaelo.' (I feel pain in my spine.)

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Every sitting instruction begins with the spine: upright without rigidity. The meditation posture needed its axis named.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The lifter's wisdom was spinal wisdom: bend the knees, keep the line — the body's engineering, taught by grandmothers.
